t_ch: 你认真数了一遍储存便携装置的立方体，发现一共有二十一个。你又数了几次，但每次的数量都不一样。
t_en: You carefully count the cubes that store the portable devices and find there are <unbreak>21</unbreak> in total. You count a few more times, but end up with a different figure each time.

t_ch: 立方体中的便携装置有被取走的痕迹，看来需要「快乐」的人真的很多。
t_en: There are signs that the portable devices have been removed from the cubes. It seems like there is a high demand for joy.

t_ch: 每个生命体都应当拥有一套完整的「分装快乐」，快乐永不嫌多。
t_en: Every living being should own a complete Joy Filtration Set. One can never have too much joy.

t_ch: 「奇物编号<unbreak>1381</unbreak>：心之全蚀。」
t_en: "Curio No. <unbreak>1381</unbreak>: Total Eclipse of the Heart."

t_ch: 锥体黯淡无光，显然处于休眠状态。
t_en: The dull and lifeless cone is obviously in a dormant state.

t_ch: 在它附近贴有警告：「未经空间站书面许可，切勿启动。」
t_en: A warning notice affixed next to it reads, "Do not activate this without written permission from the space station."

t_ch: 这是用来分解空间站废弃物品的装置，对气味敏感的人或许需要屏住呼吸。
t_en: This is a device used to decompose rubbish from the space station. Those sensitive to odors may need to hold their breath.

t_ch: 「军团」入侵留下的影响尚未消除，这里并未展出空间站所收容的全部奇物。
t_en: As the after-effects of the Legion's invasion have not been completely removed, not all Curios in the space station are displayed here.

t_ch: 应物科才收容回来的奇物，你甚至查询不到它的奇物档案。
t_en: A Curio that has just been contained by the Department of Implement Arts. Even its archival information is not yet available.

t_ch: 存放着奇物的展柜，星体上的奇物已被其他科员借走。
t_en: A display cabinet used to store Curios. The Curios on the planetary body have been borrowed by other researchers.

t_ch: （音乐）
t_en: (Music)

t_ch: （音乐）
t_en: (Music)

t_ch: 你睁开眼，从想象中醒来。黑色的羽翼依旧在飞舞，像是在诉说一段故事，像是在歌唱一个世界。
t_en: You open your eyes and wake from your imagination. The black feathered wings fly still, as if recounting a story, as if singing a whole world.

t_ch: 空气中忽然传来一阵微热，拂过脸颊，在你的耳畔驻足停步。
t_en: A gust of heat suddenly arrives through the air. It touches your cheeks and pauses next to your ear.

t_ch: 「她自火中新生，她在火中微笑。」
t_en: "She was reborn in the fire. She was smiling in the fire."

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着海瑟音：「<unbreak>0.129</unbreak>分！」它宣布。
t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Hysilens: "<unbreak>0.129</unbreak> points!" it announces.

t_ch: 眼睛敏捷地转动，「<unbreak>0.129</unbreak>分…<unbreak>0.12999</unbreak>分…<unbreak>0.129999</unbreak>分……」。恍然，眼睛停止转动，呆呆望着某处，仿佛坠入了深不见底的空洞。
t_en: The eye turns dexterously. "<unbreak>0.129</unbreak> points... <unbreak>0.12999</unbreak> points... <unbreak>0.129999</unbreak> points...". The eye suddenly stops moving, gazing blankly in a certain direction, as if fallen into a bottomless void.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着刻律德菈：「<unbreak>0.13</unbreak>分！」它宣布。
t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Cerydra: "<unbreak>0.13</unbreak> points!" it announces.

t_ch: 眼睛紧盯前方，流露出一丝不易察觉的傲气。僵持片刻，它垂低眼眸，在一场关乎气势的对决中败下阵来。
t_en: The eye keeps its gaze straight ahead, emitting a subtle and almost imperceptible pride. After a brief impasse, it lowers its gaze, admitting defeat in a duel of auras.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着丹恒•腾荒：「<unbreak>88</unbreak>分！」它宣布。
t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Dan Heng • Permansor Terrae: "<unbreak>88</unbreak> points!" it announces.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着长夜月：「<unbreak>37</unbreak>分！」它宣布。
t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Evernight: "<unbreak>37</unbreak> points!" it announces.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着昔涟：「<unbreak>0.336</unbreak>分…<unbreak>0.337</unbreak>分…<unbreak>0.999</unbreak>分…1分——」
t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Cyrene: "<unbreak>0.336</unbreak> points... <unbreak>0.337</unbreak> points... <unbreak>0.999</unbreak> points... 1 point—"

t_ch: 眼睛忽而变得闪亮，忽而变得哀伤，仿佛阅读着一页页不可思议的故事。直至某一刻，它回过神来：「无法测定！」眼睛不再回应，坚定地望向远方，就像预见了故事的下一页。
t_en: The eyeball flickers between sparkling and sorrowful, as if reading through pages of incredible stories. Until, at one moment, it snaps back to reality: "Rating invalid!" The eye stops responding, gazing firmly into the distance, as if it had glimpsed the next page of the story.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着大丽花：「<unbreak>40</unbreak>分！」它宣布。
t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at The Dahlia: "<unbreak>40</unbreak> points!" it announces.

t_ch: 「<unbreak>22</unbreak>分？！」眼珠怔住片刻，随即骨碌碌地转动两圈。
t_en: "<unbreak>22</unbreak> points!?" Its eyeball freezes for a moment, then swivels around twice in quick succession.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着火花：「哈哈！<unbreak>144</unbreak>分！」它最终宣布。
t_en: The rating pistol's eyes look at Sparxie: "Hahah! <unbreak>144</unbreak> points!" It finally announces.

t_ch: 眼睛逐渐充满笑意，欣赏、痴迷、心花怒放。恍然，它眨了眨眼睛，恢复常态。
t_en: Its eyes gradually filled with mirth, appreciation, obsession, thrilling delight. Then suddenly, it blinks, returning to normal.

t_ch: 「无法测定！」眼珠在转动了三圈后突然瞪大，「参演编号？是祂…祂果然又出现了！」眼珠颤动着，睁了又闭，闭了又睁，它已许久没有如此兴奋过了。
t_en: "Rating invalid!" Its eyeball swivels around three times before suddenly widening. "Participation number? It's THEM... THEY've appeared again, just as expected!" The eye quivers, blinking rapidly. Open, shut, open, shut. It has been ages since it felt such exhilaration.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着爻光：「哈哈！<unbreak>116</unbreak>分！」，它最终宣布。
t_en: The rating pistol's eyes look at Yao Guang: "Hahah! <unbreak>116</unbreak> points!" It finally announces.

t_ch: 眼睛弯成月牙，仿佛无数个侧面从眼前略过。许久之后，它恢复常态，不再回应。
t_en: Its eye narrows into thin slivers, as if a thousand different facets were flickering past. After a long silence, it returns to normal, offering no further response.

t_ch: 定分枪的眼睛看着不死途：「<unbreak>87</unbreak>分！」它宣布。
t_en: The Rating Pistol gazes at Ashveil: "<unbreak>87</unbreak> points!" it announces.

t_ch: 好啦，我也该回去了。
t_en: Alright, I should go back now.

t_ch: 这里…就…交给……
t_en: I'll be here, you've got to fend them off...

t_ch: 你们…尽快……
t_en: You... hurry...

t_ch: …通讯断了。
t_en: ...We lost communications.

t_ch: 要回去么？提醒一句：那是末日兽，军团的对星体兵器。
t_en: Are you thinking of going back? Let me remind you, that's the Doomsday Beast, the Legion's planet destroyer.

t_ch: 这空间站是黑塔建的。{RUBY_B#「毁灭」的令使}绝灭大君{RUBY_E#}不出手，这里不会有什么大碍。
t_en: The space station is Herta's creation. As long as a {RUBY_B#Emanator of Destruction}Lord Ravager{RUBY_E#} doesn't intervene, there should be no problems.

t_ch: 可、可咱们也不能就这么跑了吧……
t_en: B—But we can't just run away like this, right...

t_ch: 末日兽撕开防护罩易如反掌，黑塔不在，这儿的防御系统对反物质军团来说太过脆弱。
t_en: The Doomsday Beast can rip off the defense shield like tearing paper — and Herta's not here. The station's defenses are too weak to stand against the Antimatter Legion.

t_ch: 军团拥有星神{RUBY_B#「毁灭」}纳努克{RUBY_E#}的赐福。它们有备而来，这里的人是守不住的。
t_en: Either way, the Legion has the blessing of the Aeon, {RUBY_B#The Destruction}Nanook{RUBY_E#}. They came prepared and everyone here is not.

t_ch: 所以我们才必须离开——而且要带上{NICKNAME}一起走。
t_en: That's why we have to leave and take {NICKNAME} with us.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}？
t_en: {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 这样吗…{M#他}{F#她}——很关键？
t_en: {M#He's}{F#She's}... that important?

t_ch: {M#他}{F#她}是破局之人——当然，我也可能弄错。
t_en: {M#He's}{F#She's} the one who can help us turn the tides... Of course, I may be wrong.

t_ch: 星穹列车的行车仪上即时记录我们的行迹。空间站的动静这么大，瓦尔特先生不可能注意不到。
t_en: The Astral Express tracks our coordinates in real time... And with such a huge change in the space station's movements, there's no way Mr. Yang wouldn't have noticed.

t_ch: 嗯，我敢保证，你们的杨叔正在赶来的路上。
t_en: Hmm, I can almost guarantee that your Mr. Yang is already on his way.

t_ch: 现在的话，只是末日兽姑且还能应付，但如果「毁灭」的令使也来了……
t_en: Right now, we might be able to handle things if it's just the Doomsday Beast. But, if the Destruction's Emanator were to appear...

t_ch: 我…姑且应付不了…
t_en: I... can't handle...

t_ch: 「毁灭」的令使？
t_en: The Destruction's Emanator?

t_ch: 总觉得事情没那么顺利。
t_en: I still feel that things won't be going as planned.

t_ch: 先抓紧离开这里吧，之后会跟你好好解释的。
t_en: Let's hurry up and get out of here. I'll explain later.

t_ch: 出口在那边…怎么过去呢？
t_en: The exit's over there... how do we get to it?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，用配控装置转换斥力桥。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, use the control device to switch the repulsion bridge.

t_ch: 有什么飞了过去。
t_en: ...Something just flew past.

t_ch: 难道说……
t_en: Could it be...

t_ch: 不会是列车吧？看来它会比我们先到月台耶。
t_en: Could it be the Express? It looks like it'll get to the platform before us.

t_ch: 看见月台了，好耶！
t_en: Aww yeah, it's the platform!

t_ch: …列车还没到。
t_en: ...The Express isn't here.

t_ch: 保不准就在附近！我视力好，先去瞅瞅。
t_en: It has to be nearby! Let me go take a peek with my sharp eyes.

t_ch: 等等，三月！
t_en: Wait, March!

t_ch: …如果真是这样就好了。
t_en: ...I really hope that's true.

t_ch: …既如此。好吧，接下来该怎么做？
t_en: ...As you say so. Alright, what should we do next?

t_ch: 这里是检修科员工作的支援舱段，能通往最近的月台。我们去那儿，和瓦尔特会合。
t_en: This is the supply zone where the maintenance crew works. There's a path here that leads to the nearest railway platform. Let's head over there and meet up with Welt.

t_ch: 杨叔？杨叔也来了？他不是留在车上了吗？
t_en: Mr. Yang? Mr. Yang's here too? Didn't he stay on the Express?

t_ch: 朝最近的月台移动吧，与列车会合是当务之急。
t_en: Head to the nearest platform. Getting to the Express is our top priority.

t_ch: 支援舱段部分的防护罩也撑不了多久，你们尽快……
t_en: The supply zone's defense shield won't hold much longer. You all need to hurry...

t_ch: 这、这也太大了…喂，我叫你下来你就下来啊？！
t_en: I—It's bigger than I thought... Hey you, I was just joking! Leave us alone, shoo!

t_ch: 先别闹，攻击要来了！
t_en: Incoming, look out!

t_ch: 这种力量的流动，不太寻常。
t_en: This energy flow... it's up to something.

t_ch: 那个右手上似乎附着什么糟糕的东西，都打起精神来！
t_en: There's something on its right hand, stay alert!

t_ch: 趴着吧您嘞！看见没，这就是挑衅本姑娘的下场！
t_en: Gotcha! See, that's what you get for annoying me!

t_ch: 不对，军团的对星体兵器，不应该这么简单。
t_en: No, the Legion's weapon shouldn't go down so easily.

t_ch: 麻烦的事一会儿再想。先集火，以免夜长梦多。
t_en: Let's worry about that later. We need to focus fire — fast.

t_ch: 大家都还好么？
t_en: Is everybody okay?

t_ch: 不打了不打了，这大家伙彻底炸毛啦！
t_en: I'm out, I'm out! It's gone bananas!

t_ch: 这破坏力…看来，它终于要动真格了。
t_en: Not good... It's ready to throw everything at us.

t_ch: 喂喂喂，感觉…它是不是在憋什么大招？
t_en: Hey! Is it trying to pull a charged attack?

t_ch: 这还用感觉？力量在汇聚，大的要来了！
t_en: I don't see any other possibilities. Here we go, brace yourselves!

t_ch: （这里是…空间站？）
t_en: (Is this... the space station?)

t_ch: 嗨～
t_en: Hello there~

t_ch: 嗨。
t_en: Hi.

t_ch: 你醒了。我让小三月他们留在列车附近照看一下。
t_en: You're awake. I had March and Dan Heng stay by the Express to keep an eye on things.

t_ch: 时间差不多，我等的人应该快到了。
t_en: It's almost time. She should be arriving any moment now.

t_ch: 我才走了几个月啊？嗯？空间站就搞成了这个德性？
t_en: I've only been gone, what? A few months? And the space station is already in this state?

t_ch: 你回来啦，黑塔。
t_en: Welcome back, Herta.

t_ch: 这位就是空间站真正的主人，「天才俱乐部」#83，黑塔。
t_en: This is the true master of the space station — Genius Society number <unbreak>83</unbreak>, Herta.

t_ch: 介绍我就好好介绍，提什么俱乐部？我那么多非凡成就，哪个不比#83好听？
t_en: At least give me a proper introduction! Genius Society number <unbreak>83</unbreak>... Of all my outstanding achievements, that's what you want to mention?

t_ch: 空间站…黑塔…？
t_en: Space station... Herta...?

t_ch: 对啊，你现在才反应过来？你们搞得天翻地覆的地方，是我的私人财产。
t_en: Yeah, up to speed yet? That place you all turned upside down? That's my property.

t_ch: 你是…机器人？
t_en: You're... a robot?

t_ch: 你看见的这个是我做的人偶，我操纵她和你们对话。
t_en: What you're seeing here is one of my puppets. I'm using her to talk to you.

t_ch: 噗，天才俱乐部…
t_en: Heh, Genius Society...

t_ch: 想笑就笑吧，赞达尔那老鬼起的名字，你当我喜欢吗？
t_en: Alright, laugh it up. That old Zandar came up with the name. You think I like it?

t_ch: 现在「星核」是这个小鬼了？
t_en: So this little twerp has the Stellaron now? Huh.

t_ch: 哼哼，真是神了，为了拘束这颗未启动的星核，让湛蓝星免于灾祸，我造了一整座太空站……
t_en: Hmm, truly amazing! I built a whole space station just to contain this unactivated Stellaron and keep The Blue from disaster...

t_ch: 有人却返璞归真，用这小鬼一具身体就搞定了——怎么做到的？
t_en: Yet someone was able to achieve that with this little twerp's body. How did they do it?

t_ch: 而且「星核」在{NICKNAME}体内还很稳定……
t_en: Moreover, the Stellaron is still very stable in {NICKNAME}'s body.

t_ch: 是啊，这小家伙的体质真奇怪……
t_en: You're right. This little one's body truly is strange...

t_ch: 我不是小家伙。
t_en: Don't call me "little one."

t_ch: 哈，跟我比，你不就是小家伙？你才几岁啊？
t_en: Hah! Compared to me, you are. How old are you even?

t_ch: 人不可貌相啊，黑塔，你这具身体看着比{NICKNAME}小多了。
t_en: Don't judge a book by its cover, Herta. Case in point, that puppet body of yours is actually much smaller than {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我不一样…咦，这家伙该不会和你们那个三月七一个类型吧？那……
t_en: That's... beside the point. Hey is this one the same kind as that March 7th? If that were true...

t_ch: 我叫{NICKNAME}。
t_en: My name is {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 行，{NICKNAME}，我知道了。不过我还是会叫你小鬼，我的大脑太宝贵了，没空存别人的名字。
t_en: Alright, {NICKNAME}. I got it. But I'm still gonna call you little twerp. The space in my brain is too valuable to store peoples' names.

t_ch: 那谢谢你记得我喔。
t_en: Oh! Well, thanks for remembering my name then.

t_ch: 那不一样，你我有商业往来——你叫什么来着？
t_en: That's different. We have business with each other. Umm, what was your name again?

t_ch: 算了，说回这个体内能存星核的小家伙……
t_en: Forget it, let's focus on the one who can store a Stellaron in their body...

t_ch: 你们非得当面讨论我吗？
t_en: Must you two discuss this right in front of me?

t_ch: 你可以走开啊。我在和——你叫什么来着？姬子？——讨论科学。科学是客观存在的，没什么好避讳。
t_en: You can leave. I'm discussing science with what's her face... Himeko? Science exists in objectivity, there's nothing to be secretive about.

t_ch: 就你的处境而言，我觉得你在场听听是件好事。毕竟我有一大堆和你有关的计划，说到这里，姬子……
t_en: But as far as you're concerned, I think it would be better for you to listen in. After all, I have a lot of plans ahead involving you. Speaking of which, Himeko...

t_ch: 我能拿{M#他}{F#她}做点研究吗？
t_en: Can I bring {M#him}{F#her} in for some research?

t_ch: 这我可不能做主，你得问{NICKNAME}自己……
t_en: That's not up to me to decide. You can ask {NICKNAME} yourself...

t_ch: 没门儿！
t_en: No way!

t_ch: 这小鬼，脾气还挺大。放心，不会让你受一丁点伤害。凭我的本事，抽血都伤不着你的血管。
t_en: This little twerp really has a temper. Don't worry, I won't hurt a hair on your head. With my skills, even surgery would be painless!

t_ch: 你就不担心自己身体里藏着颗炸弹会怎么样吗？让我来帮你吧，也就是现在我对你还有点兴趣，等我失去热情了，你求我我都不研究你咯。
t_en: Aren't you worried about that bomb inside of you? Let me help you while I'm still interested. Once I'm not, I won't do any studies even if you beg me.

t_ch: 啥研究？
t_en: Study what?

t_ch: 研究你啊——你身体里有颗星核，跟藏着颗炸弹也没区别了。谁知道它有什么危害？以后会不会突然爆发，坑你一把？
t_en: Study you of course. Your body contains a Stellaron, which, in some sense, is no different than storing a bomb. Who knows what might happen? Maybe it'll blow you to bits someday.

t_ch: 现在本天才愿意出手相助，你就谢恩吧！也就是现在我对你还有点兴趣，等我失去热情了，你求我我都不研究你咯。
t_en: You should be grateful that this genius is willing to help you out. I still have some interest now, but once that's gone, I'm not studying you even if you beg me.

t_ch: 多少钱？
t_en: What's your price?

t_ch: 这小鬼还挺有经济头脑，我蛮中意的。
t_en: This little twerp's pretty financially motivated. I like it.

t_ch: 你别太欺负人家了。
t_en: Alright, give the poor child a break, Herta.

t_ch: 我现在感兴趣，什么都愿意迁就。身体里有颗星核哎，多有意思！谢恩吧！我出手帮你，这可是星际和平公司花钱都请不着的服务。
t_en: I'm very interested now, so there's almost nothing I won't accommodate. A Stellaron in your body, how interesting is that! Be grateful that I'm offering to help you out. This is a service even the IPC can't buy.

t_ch: 明白了吗，{NICKNAME}，黑塔想让你留在她的空间站呢。
t_en: You understand now, {NICKNAME}? Herta wants you to stay in her space station.

t_ch: 别，要严谨：只留一阵子——我做完研究，或者做到一半没兴趣了，这小鬼就得走人。
t_en: Well, I'm going to have to modify your wording here: This little twerp can only stay temporarily until the research is done. Or maybe I'll lose interest halfway through and they can just beat it.

t_ch: 之后呢？
t_en: And after that?

t_ch: 之后关我什么事？
t_en: Not my problem.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…你知道你还有另一个选择，月台上停着一辆星穹列车。如果你愿意，可以跟我们一起走。
t_en: {NICKNAME}... You also have another option — the Astral Express. If you want, you can leave with us.

t_ch: 列车和「星核」没少打交道。你所担忧的，也是我们在寻求的答案。再者说……
t_en: The Express has its fair share of experiences with Stellarons. The thing you're worried about, and the answers we're looking for are one and the same. Besides...

t_ch: 我们随时可以回来让黑塔做研究呀，她现在兴趣满满呢。
t_en: We can come back any time to let Herta conduct her research. She's absolutely fascinated now.

t_ch: 这倒不错，还多少能保持点新鲜感。用不着这小鬼的时候，我也不用操这个心。
t_en: Hm, works for me, keeps this subject fresh, too. And that way I won't need to keep worrying about this little twerp all the time. Perfect!

t_ch: 要么上列车，要么留下…
t_en: Get on the Express, or stay behind...

t_ch: 我是建议你上列车啦。你留下对我有那么一点好处，但总得来说没什么帮助。
t_en: My advice would be to get on. You're not doing me that big of a favor by staying anyway.

t_ch: 你真是我见过最自私的人。
t_en: You really are the most self-centered person I've ever met.

t_ch: 那你真该见见天才俱乐部的其他几位，有的卖了你你还得说声谢谢。而我至少想什么就说什么。
t_en: You should meet the others of the Genius Society. Some of them will trick you into thanking them after they milk you dry. At least I have my honesty going for me.

t_ch: 我会考虑姬子的邀请…
t_en: I'll think about Himeko's offer...

t_ch: 是啊，你好好考虑一下。
t_en: Yes, give it some good thought.

t_ch: 走了记得常回来看看～提前预个约，找艾丝妲或者阿兰就行，我好把时间空下来研究你。
t_en: Just remember to come back often. Make an appointment in advance with Asta or Arlan so I can make time to study you.

t_ch: 别急，黑塔，艾丝妲也在主控舱段，让{NICKNAME}也和艾丝妲聊聊，好好考虑一下。
t_en: There's no need to rush into this, Herta. Asta's in the master control zone. Let's let {NICKNAME} have a talk with Asta first and decide for {M#himself}{F#herself}.

t_ch: 我在月台等你，{NICKNAME}。你还有事情想做，有人想见吧？不用着急。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I'll be waiting for you on the platform. It's no hurry if you still have things to do or someone to see.

t_ch: 决定好了就来找我。
t_en: Come find me when you've made your decision.

t_ch: 哼，那我得好好看看。
t_en: Hmm, I'll have to take a good look.

t_ch: 还有疑惑？好啊，我对你有兴趣，愿意回答你的问题。
t_en: Still haven't made up your mind? Fine. Since I'm interested in you, I'll answer any questions you have.

t_ch: 关于「黑塔」…
t_en: About Herta...

t_ch: 你是空间站的主人？
t_en: You're the master of the space station?

t_ch: 我是法律上的主人，实质上这个地方我不管。我造好它，往里面放东西，塞人，然后让人自己管理。
t_en: On paper, yes. But I don't really care about this place. I built it, put some stuff in, left some people here, and let them just manage themselves.

t_ch: 空间站的站长是艾丝妲，严格来说她才是主人。我拒绝「拥有」这个概念，因为我不想负任何责任。
t_en: Asta's the lead researcher of the space station, so for all intents and purposes, she's the master. I reject the concept of ownership because I don't want the responsibilities that come with it.

t_ch: 你的性格真的很烂。
t_en: You really are a terrible person.

t_ch: 无所谓，我在乎别人喜欢我吗？答案是不在乎。性格烂又怎么样，只要我有价值，就能心想事成。
t_en: It doesn't matter. Who cares if others don't like me? I know I don't care. As long as I have my own value, I can do whatever I want.

t_ch: 你的性格倒是挺好的，有什么价值吗？——别误会，我不是在嘲讽你。这是个疑问句，就是说，它可能真的有价值，只是我不知道。
t_en: While you may have good character, do you have any value? Don't take that the wrong way, I'm genuinely asking you. You might have some value, I just don't know about it.

t_ch: 你的本体长什么样？
t_en: What do you actually look like?

t_ch: 人类，女性，年轻，貌美，可爱。
t_en: Human, female, young, beautiful, attractive.

t_ch: 和你现在看见的这具身体差不多，我按小时候的样子造的，也许不是很还原，但总有七八分相像吧。
t_en: Not much different from what you're seeing now. I modeled it after how I looked, back when I was a kid. It might not be identical, but I'd say maybe <unbreak>70</unbreak>, <unbreak>80</unbreak> percent there?

t_ch: 唉，那时候的我已经过分早熟了，就知道解孤波难题，忽略了宝贵的童真时代……
t_en: Oh, I was so precocious — all I cared about back then was solving the solitary wave problem, ignoring my precious childhood...

t_ch: 我还有别的问题…
t_en: I still have some questions...

t_ch: 关于「星核」…
t_en: About Stellarons...

t_ch: 「星核」到底是什么？
t_en: What exactly is a Stellaron?

t_ch: 根据我的研究，它是一种生命体；一种具有特殊机制，连通某条命途或某个星神力量的特殊物质。
t_en: According to my research, it's a type of lifeform with a special property that tethers it to a certain Path or Aeon's power.

t_ch: 它会回应所在世界擢升文明的欲望，但实现愿望的形式总是某种灾祸。其机制很可能与「毁灭」的纳努克有关。
t_en: Wherever it's located, it responds to that world's desires to advance its civilization. But, this comes at the cost of some form of disaster. The mechanism behind it is likely related to Nanook the Destruction.

t_ch: 你知道星核猎手吗？
t_en: Do you know about the Stellaron Hunters?

t_ch: 噢，一群怪人。我得到一颗星核就满足了，他们在宇宙中搜刮了好几颗仍不满足，不知道他们的真实意图是什么。
t_en: Oh, that weird bunch. I'm satisfied with just one Stellaron, but they've scoured the universe, obtained several, and still aren't satisfied. I don't know what their true intentions are.

t_ch: 俱乐部的{RUBY_B#天才俱乐部#76}螺丝咕姆{RUBY_E#}曾经和他们对过一局，听说有个成员的骇客水平非常高，能跟螺丝咕姆的差分机球打个平手。
t_en: {RUBY_B#Genius Society #76}Screwllum{RUBY_E#} went up against the Stellaron Hunters before. I heard there was a member whose hacking skills were on par with Screwllum.

t_ch: 能媲美螺丝咕姆——那在宇宙里也是数一数二了！真厉害啊，我不行，在计算机领域不算很出色。
t_en: Being on the same level as Screwllum — who's probably the best in the universe — is nothing short of spectacular. It's beyond my abilities, computers are not my forte.

t_ch: 为什么我能容纳「星核」？
t_en: Why am I able to host the Stellaron?

t_ch: 不知道啊，我正摩拳擦掌，要解开这个谜呢。
t_en: Ha... That I don't know. Which is why I'm gearing up to solve this mystery.

t_ch: 我还有别的问题…
t_en: I still have some questions...

t_ch: 关于「列车」…
t_en: About the Express...

t_ch: 星穹列车是怎样的？
t_en: What's the Astral Express like?

t_ch: 外形？就是辆列车啊。内在嘛……
t_en: You mean how it looks? Like a train. Well...

t_ch: 有阵子我对列车很感兴趣，写了不少研究报告，忘记丢哪儿了，现在什么都不记得了。
t_en: I was very interested in the Express for a while, and wrote a lot of papers, but forgot where I left them. Now I can't remember anything about it.

t_ch: 你信任星穹列车吗？
t_en: Do you trust the Astral Express?

t_ch: 信任啊。
t_en: Uh-huh.

t_ch: 你们是什么关系？
t_en: What's your relationship with them?

t_ch: 合作很久的商业伙伴。
t_en: We've been business partners for a long time.

t_ch: 我经常委托他们在其他星球帮我弄点东西，他们也经常来找我解决点难题。
t_en: Every once in a while, I'll need them to get me things from other planets, and they often come to me to help them solve problems.

t_ch: 我还有别的问题…
t_en: I still have some questions...

t_ch: 关于「选择」…
t_en: About the decision...

t_ch: 我还能回空间站吗？
t_en: Can I return to the space station?

t_ch: 当然能呀，我还等着你呢——噢，你说的「回」是——噢，倒也不是不行。
t_en: Of course, I'll still be here waiting for you, eh... and the space station will be waiting for you. Returning... it's not impossible.

t_ch: 你愿意在这儿当个普通科员的话，可以问问艾丝妲，她同意我就没意见。
t_en: If you wanna be a researcher here, you can just ask Asta. If she's fine with it, I have no complaints.

t_ch: 不，等下——你身体有颗星核啊——你还是走吧，偶尔回来瞧瞧，久住还是太危险了。
t_en: No, hold on — you have a Stellaron within you. I think it's better if you left and came back occasionally. It might be dangerous if you stay for too long...

t_ch: 我应该登上列车吗？
t_en: So I should board the Express?

t_ch: 从绝对理性的角度分析判断，星穹列车是你最好的去处。
t_en: Well, from an objective standpoint, that's the most rational place for you to be.

t_ch: 首先有地方住；其次有人保护；再其次路途遥远，救命的机会比较多；最后，就算星核真炸了也害不了太多人。
t_en: First of all, you have somewhere to live. And you'd be under their protection. Also, it'll be a long trip, so you'll have more opportunities to find a way to save yourself from the Stellaron. Lastly, if the Stellaron really explodes, you won't harm too many people around you.

t_ch: 我还有别的选择吗？
t_en: Do I have any other choice?

t_ch: 这我怎么知道！看你咯，选择总是很多的，你也可以找个没人的地方打开舱门跳出去；或者钻进裂界，看看怪物会不会把你当神膜拜起来。
t_en: Oh, how should I know! It's up to you, you have so many options to choose from. Maybe jump out a hatch, or hop into the Fragmentum and see if any monsters start worshiping you?

t_ch: 我还有别的问题…
t_en: I still have some questions...

t_ch: 我再想想。
t_en: Let me think things over.

t_ch: 嗯，随便你。
t_en: Okay, suit yourself.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，幸好你们都没事。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, thank goodness you're all safe.

t_ch: 可惜还没好好聊上几句，列车却要出发了，你还有什么想和我说的吗？
t_en: It's a pity that we haven't had the chance to talk much, and now the Express is about to leave. Do you have any questions?

t_ch: 空间站受损严重么？
t_en: Is the damage to the space station serious?

t_ch: 黑塔女士给了我代码——据说是她在来的路上写的，我刚刚试了下，防护罩的修复速度比之前快了三倍。
t_en: Madam Herta gave me a piece of code, which she apparently wrote on the way here. I just tried it out and the defense shield's regenerating three times faster than before.

t_ch: 托她的福，外面的反物质军团算不上什么威胁，空间站的修复重建工作也可以顺利启动，只是内部……
t_en: Thanks to her, the Antimatter Legion outside is not much of a threat anymore. The space station's repairs and reconstruction can also begin smoothly, but internally...

t_ch: 「星核」被取出的一瞬间，虽然只有一瞬间，还是侵蚀了空间站，形成一种被称作「裂界」的新空间。
t_en: When the Stellaron was extracted, even for that brief moment, it corroded the space station and formed a new space — what we now call the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 被侵蚀的部分不可逆转，因此空间站恐怕会一直处于半「裂界」化的状态……
t_en: The part that was corroded is irreversible, so the space station will always be in a state of quasi-Fragmentum.

t_ch: 「裂界」会产生什么影响？
t_en: What are the effects of the Fragmentum?

t_ch: 各个世界的「裂界」彼此相通，因此怪物会通过「裂界」来到空间站内。
t_en: All existing Fragmentums are connected, so there will be monsters that will enter the space station through its Fragmentum.

t_ch: 我也会被「裂界」侵蚀么？
t_en: Will I also be corroded by the Fragmentum?

t_ch: 哎，我也想知道这个问题的答案。
t_en: Ahh, I'd like to know the answer to that as well.

t_ch: 各个世界的「裂界」彼此相通，因此怪物会通过「裂界」来到空间站内。但没东西从你身体里钻出来，所以……
t_en: All existing Fragmentums are connected, so monsters will be able to enter the space station through the Fragmentum here. But it shouldn't allow anything to exit your body, so...

t_ch: 我和阿兰聊过了，这事儿目前没有解决的好法子，只能增加安保人员，提高巡逻密度，保障科员的研究。
t_en: I talked with Arlan and there's no easy solution to this problem at the moment. We can only increase the security and patrol frequency to protect the ongoing research.

t_ch: 我能留在空间站吗？
t_en: Can I stay at the space station?

t_ch: 我挺中意你的，但…你身体里有一颗「星核」。
t_en: I like you, but... you have a Stellaron in your body.

t_ch: 如果你只是在这随便走走逛逛，或者同意被封在实验舱里，那没什么问题。
t_en: If you were just wandering around, or if you agreed to be sealed away in an experiment pod, that would be fine.

t_ch: 但如果你要在空间站工作，长期居住，「星核」可能会让空间站的「裂界」化更加严重…作为站长，我不能冒这个险。
t_en: But if you're going to work in the space station long-term, the Stellaron may have some serious effects on the station's Fragmentum. As the lead researcher, that's a risk I cannot take.

t_ch: 我也能感受到你对空间站的热情啦，不过还是得狠心拒绝你。
t_en: I can sense your enthusiasm for the space station, but I'm afraid I have to turn you away.

t_ch: 真的不需要我帮忙么？
t_en: Is there really no way I can help?

t_ch: 你想回来帮忙，我当然不会拒绝。丹恒帮我们重新清点了奇物，三月七说她会在力所能及的范围内帮忙，于是在电力室接了五十多根电线…哎，列车的人都这么好心……
t_en: If you wanted to come back and help, of course, I wouldn't refuse you. Dan Heng helped inventory our rare items, March 7th said that she would help out as much as she can and connected over <unbreak>50</unbreak> wires in the electrical room. Everyone from the Express is so obliging...

t_ch: 就「多管闲事」这一点，你也很有潜质啊，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: And I think you're pretty good at "meddling" too, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我再想想。
t_en: Let me think things over.

t_ch: 好呀，不急。
t_en: Sure, no hurry.

t_ch: 我？谈论自己是最无意义的行为。
t_en: Me? Hmm, well, Talking about oneself is as pointless as it gets.

t_ch: 列车的事，你不问姬子，倒来问我？
t_en: The Express? Shouldn't you be asking Himeko instead?

t_ch: 站长也要注意休息。
t_en: Make sure you don't burn yourself out.

t_ch: 这阵忙完，一定得和黑塔女士申请好好休假！我已经想好了，要包个飞行器，把湛蓝星看个够。
t_en: After all this is over, I'm putting my vacation time in with Madam Herta. I've got it all planned. I'll hop on an airship and give The Blue a good look.

t_ch: 在空间站也能看到吧。
t_en: You can see it from the space station, you know?

t_ch: 就算景色一样，休假时看和工作时看，当然是两种心情啦。
t_en: Be that as it may, taking it in while on vacation and observing it during work are not the same thing.

t_ch: 黑塔女士会批休假吗？
t_en: Will Madam Herta let you take a vacation?

t_ch: 应该会吧？但我来空间站后好像还没申请过休假……
t_en: I guess so? I haven't taken leave once since I joined the space station...

t_ch: 如果有一段完整的假期，我可以慢慢收集观测数据和写完论文了。
t_en: And if I have an actual, full vacation, I can take my time to collect observational data and finish writing my paper.

t_ch: 湛蓝星的资料当然要多少有多少，但我还是想自己研究一下。虽然听着和工作内容差不多，但这真的是兴趣，没有任何指标——而且，我自费的嘛。
t_en: Of course, there's already a lot of published research on The Blue, but I want to study it myself. It might sound a lot like work, but it's really just a hobby — there are no deadlines or quotas to meet, and I don't get paid.

t_ch: 星核啊…你问吧，我听着。
t_en: Stellarons, huh? Ask away, I'm listening.

t_ch: 什么「选择」？说清楚一点。
t_en: Uh, what decision? You're gonna have to be more specific.

t_ch: 嗨，{NICKNAME}，我都听说了。你要和我们一起上列车，对吧？
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}! I heard the news. You're coming with us on the Express, right?

t_ch: 我还没决定呢！
t_en: I haven't decided yet!

t_ch: 唉呀，你打心底就是这么想的——在我面前就别装了，我都看得出来。
t_en: Oh come on, you know that's what you want deep down inside. I can see it on your face.

t_ch: 你希望我来吗？
t_en: Do you want me to come along?

t_ch: 那还用说么？这一路过来，咱俩很合得来，还是同龄人。光这两点，就够不容易了。
t_en: Do I even need to say it? I think we're becoming fast friends... isn't that enough?

t_ch: 不过你看起来还有什么烦恼，来，你说，我听着。
t_en: But you look like you've got something on your mind... come on, out with it!

t_ch: 时间过得真快啊。
t_en: Oh how time flies.

t_ch: 感慨来得太突然了吧！
t_en: Getting nostalgic already?

t_ch: …不过，回忆起在收容舱段的时候，我还在想这孩子晕过去一定是身体很虚弱啦，所以得好好照顾你，没想到你捡了根棒球棍就生龙活虎起来了。
t_en: Y'know, when you were passed out, all I thought was I had to look after you. And then you picked up the bat! Just incredible!

t_ch: 再一眨眼，你就把那大家伙给打进杨叔的黑洞里，还救了我…我还没向你好好道谢……
t_en: In the blink of an eye, you knocked that big fella into Mr. Yang's black hole and saved me... I still haven't thanked you properly yet...

t_ch: 唉，不会就要在这儿分开了吧……
t_en: Nah, we're not splitting up here...

t_ch: 你是怎么上车的呢？
t_en: How did you join the Express?

t_ch: 这问题我可有发言权啦，因为我一醒来，就在车上啦。
t_en: I'd like to know too, because I was already on the Express when I woke up.

t_ch: 我在宇宙中漂流，然后就被列车捞了上来，神奇吧？
t_en: I was just drifting out there in space and got picked up by the Express. Amazing, right?

t_ch: 你们常碰到这种事？
t_en: Does that kind of thing happen often?

t_ch: 比如去沙漠里捉一种名为「沙鱼」的动物，或者是矫正重力场，把一座上下颠倒的城堡恢复原貌，以及为了避开在路间睡觉的驯鹿，差点被一个从天而降的方铅矿球砸中脑袋——
t_en: For example, going to the desert to catch something called a "sandfish," correcting a gravitational field to flip an upside-down castle, or almost getting my head cracked open by a galena ball falling from the sky while trying to avoid a sleeping reindeer on the road—

t_ch: 唔…现在想想，好像也挺惊险的哦…可能因为和大家在一起，所以有任何问题基本都不是什么问题啦。
t_en: Huh... looking back now, it all seems quite dangerous... But I had the crew there with me, so the problems didn't seem all that bad.

t_ch: 我在想要不要上车…
t_en: I'm thinking about if I should get on the Express...

t_ch: 当然要呀，为什么不呢？
t_en: Of course you should! Why wouldn't you?

t_ch: 咱们现在只有四个人，再加一个帕姆，一节车厢睡一个人都管够，你忍心看着车厢空空荡荡么？
t_en: There's only four of us now, plus Pom-Pom. Each of us can have our own carriage to sleep in! You wouldn't have the heart to leave your carriage unmanned, would you?

t_ch: 这个问题得你们自己解决。
t_en: That sounds more like a problem for you guys.

t_ch: 加入我们，{NICKNAME}，这个问题马上就能解决。
t_en: Join us, {NICKNAME}. Then it wouldn't be a problem anymore.

t_ch: 我不知道车厢是什么样的。
t_en: I don't know what the carriage is like.

t_ch: 那就来看呀，上车就知道了。
t_en: Then come aboard and take a look for yourself.

t_ch: 我再想想。
t_en: Let me think things over.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我等你哦。
t_en: I'll be waiting for you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 是你啊，{NICKNAME}…你似乎有话要说？
t_en: Oh, it's you, {NICKNAME}... Do you have something on your mind?

t_ch: 你挺会照顾人的。
t_en: You're really good at taking care of others.

t_ch: 怎么了？我做什么了？
t_en: Oh? What did I do?

t_ch: 你每次都冲在最前。
t_en: You're always the first one to charge into battle.

t_ch: 是这样么？我是列车的护卫，本能罢了。
t_en: Is that so? Well, I am the train's guard. I suppose it's just instinct.

t_ch: 你的判断都很准确。
t_en: Your judgments are always accurate.

t_ch: 时刻注意观察，进而谨慎地选择有效事项进行思考而已，你也能做得到。
t_en: It's about being observant at all times and carefully making the best choice for the situation. You can do it too.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我觉得你也做得不错。
t_en: I think you're not bad yourself, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你好像对我不满意。
t_en: I didn't think you approved of me.

t_ch: 如果指的是对你乘上星穹列车的看法…你想多了。
t_en: If you're referring to what I think about you joining the Astral Express... You're overthinking it.

t_ch: 我的态度很简单：无所谓。建议你也不用考虑我的想法，做你自己想要的选择。
t_en: My position is simple: It doesn't matter to me. I suggest you ignore my opinions and make your own choice.

t_ch: 你不像是喜欢旅行的人。
t_en: You don't seem like someone who likes to travel.

t_ch: 坐上列车并不完全等同于「旅行」或者「冒险」…星穹列车代表的是「开拓」，知道吗？
t_en: Being on the Express isn't exactly the same as "traveling" or "adventuring..." The Astral Express symbolizes "trailblazing" you understand?

t_ch: 「开拓」往往意味着「未知」，无论是未知的风险，还是未知的乐趣。
t_en: Trailblazing implies the unknown, be it unknown risks or unknown rewards.

t_ch: 在我看来，「未知」并不比「已知」可怖，「未知」…往往也表示尚能掌控与改变。
t_en: In my opinion, the unknown is no more terrifying than the known... In fact, the unknown signifies something we can control and change.

t_ch: 我有些犹豫。
t_en: I'm a little hesitant.

t_ch: 别把犹豫当作借口。犹豫的时间久了，你原本的想法也会随之消失。
t_en: Don't use hesitation as an excuse. If you hesitate for too long, your original way of thinking will eventually cease to exist.

t_ch: 我明白。
t_en: I understand.

t_ch: 嗯，那很好。
t_en: Mm, okay then.

t_ch: 我再想想。
t_en: Let me think things over.

t_ch: 丹恒微微颔首，他的视线转向别处。
t_en: Dan Heng gives a slight nod and shifts his gaze elsewhere.

t_ch: 怎么样，想好了么？
t_en: So, have you thought things through?

t_ch: 我想上车。
t_en: I want to join the Express.

t_ch: 跟我来吧。
t_en: Then come with me.

t_ch: 谢谢你。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 不用那么正式啊，你也帮我们打败了那个大家伙，要是咱们还要讲客套话，聊到天黑都说不完了。
t_en: There's no need to be so formal. You helped us defeat that big fella, anyway. Plus, If we keep speaking pleasantries, we'll waste a whole day.

t_ch: 你帮了我一个大忙：让空间站免于反物质军团的毒手。黑塔虽然不会自认欠我一份人情，但之后我有什么请求，她也不会拒绝我——
t_en: You've done me a huge favor by defending the space station from the Antimatter Legion. And know that while Herta may not admit it, she owes me one. And she will not refuse me if I ask for a favor in return...

t_ch: 从空间站里要一件奇物，大概也没问题吧……
t_en: And it's probably not too much to ask for a rare item from the space station, right?

t_ch: 要不现在就上车聊聊，我给你泡杯咖啡——独门手艺。
t_en: How about we continue talking on the Express? I'll make you a cup of coffee, my special blend.

t_ch: 列车接下来会去哪里？
t_en: What's the next stop for the Express?

t_ch: 一如既往，追随阿基维利的足迹。
t_en: As always, to follow in the footsteps of Akivili.

t_ch: 过去，我们已经停靠了好几个世界，将来，我们也会如此继续——银河里有数不清的下一站。
t_en: Sometimes, we stop on other worlds and we'll continue to do so. There are countless "next stops" in the galaxy.

t_ch: 星辰大海不仅是我的梦想，也是列车的心愿，我渴望探索新世界，而列车希望重回它过去的航路。
t_en: I love the vast reaches of space, and the Express does too. I want to seek out new worlds, and the Express wants to return to its former path.

t_ch: 为什么邀请我上车？
t_en: Why invite me to join?

t_ch: 因为你和其他人不一样。
t_en: Because you're different.

t_ch: 又是因为星核么？
t_en: Because of the Stellaron?

t_ch: 「星核」是首要原因，但也不是唯一的原因。
t_en: While that might be the biggest reason, it's not the only one.

t_ch: 我很有自知之明。
t_en: I'm very self-aware.

t_ch: 嗯，身体里有颗「星核」这件事，不是人人都能办到的。
t_en: Well, the fact that you have a Stellaron in your body is not something most people can boast about.

t_ch: 我觉得你需要一个机会，去发现自己与其他人相比有哪些不同之处。大家都告诉你你是多么的独特，你体内有颗「星核」……
t_en: I think you need a chance. A chance to discover just how different you really are from everyone else. Everybody keeps telling you about how special you are, and how you have a Stellaron inside you...

t_ch: 但你自己清楚地认识到这一点，和别人告诉你——不管这说法是来自我还是黑塔，不管这说法看上去有多么可信——这两者是完全不同的。
t_en: But that's already plain as day to you. And no matter who tells you — be it me, or Herta, or anybody — it's not the same thing as feeling it yourself.

t_ch: 你得经历得够多，才能知道你是否因「星核」获得了什么，又失去了什么，以及你自己到底是谁…学会如何掌控这颗「星核」，才能掌控自己的命运。
t_en: You have to experience enough to know if you've gained or lost anything because of the Stellaron, and to know who you really are... Learn to control the Stellaron and then you can control your destiny.

t_ch: 「星核」对你而言或许还是未知，但在你身体内这件事已是既定事实，你得牢牢地抓住这一点，才能走向更遥远的未来。
t_en: The Stellaron might still be an enigma, but the fact of the matter is — it's a part of you. And you have to embrace this before you can move forward into the future.

t_ch: 我没有想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 还想和姬子聊聊。
t_en: I still have something to talk to you about, Himeko.

t_ch: 我还得再想想……
t_en: I need some time to think...

t_ch: 这么惊险的情况毕竟是少数啦，我也差点吓坏了。
t_en: Crazy stuff like that? Not too often. It sure freaked me out.

t_ch: 不过我跟着列车碰到的事都很千奇百怪啦。
t_en: But every encounter I've had since coming aboard the Express has been strange to say the least.

t_ch: 你的事都打理完了么？
t_en: Have you taken care of everything you need?

t_ch: 哈哈，早就打点完了。我已经来过这儿好多回了，对我来说，这本来只是一场普通的旅途而已。
t_en: Haha, I was ready ages ago. *sigh* I've been here so many times before, it should've been just an ordinary trip for me.

t_ch: 但现在，一切已经没那么普通了。
t_en: But everything's not so ordinary anymore, is it?

t_ch: 即使是重走已有的航路，仍旧会有新的收获，这就是「开拓」的意义吧。
t_en: Walking the same path over and over will never be the same, there will always be something new. That's the meaning of trailblazing.

t_ch: 嗯，想说什么都可以。
t_en: Hm? Go ahead, speak your mind.

t_ch: 打得真不错，要是自己不昏倒就更帅啦。但多亏了你，末日兽一声不吭就没了，一点脾气都没有。
t_en: Not bad. It would've been even better if you didn't faint. But thanks to you, the Doomsday Beast was as tame as a kitten. It had no temper at all.

t_ch: 我们赢了吗？
t_en: Did we win?

t_ch: 对呀，你是最大的功臣呢～
t_en: Yes, and you are the hero.

t_ch: 强如末日兽，也不过手下败…
t_en: Even the Doomsday Beast, is no match...

t_ch: 行了行了，刚夸你一句而已就喘上啦。不过嘛，你确实是最大的功臣没错。
t_en: Alright, alright. I just gave you a compliment, don't let it go to your head. However, you are indeed the hero this time.

t_ch: 我是谁，我在哪…
t_en: Who am I, where am I...

t_ch: 呃，你是开玩笑还是认真的……
t_en: Er, you are kidding, right...?

t_ch: 没事了就起来吧，姬子姐让你醒了就去找她。我还有别的事，就不陪你去啦。
t_en: Get up when you feel better. Himeko wants you to go find her. I have something to do, so I won't go with you, alright?

t_ch: 对了，交换下信标吧——要是迷路了什么的，随时发短信给我。
t_en: By the way, we should exchange beacons. If you get lost or something, feel free to text me.

t_ch: 你和三月七交换了手机号码。
t_en: You exchanged your cell phone number with March 7th.

t_ch: 好～我去忙啦，再见！
t_en: Okay, off I go. Bye!

t_ch: 你没事就好。军团的威胁已经解除，你可以放心了。
t_en: It's good that you've made it through in one piece. You can rest easy now that the threat from the Legion has been neutralized.

t_ch: 我们赢了吗？
t_en: Did we win?

t_ch: 嗯，这次的入侵似乎只是零星不成气候的骚扰。末日兽倒下后，军团就撤退了。
t_en: Well, this invasion seems to be nothing more than a random provocation. As soon as the Doomsday Beast fell, the Legion retreated.

t_ch: 强如末日兽，也不过手下败…
t_en: Even the Doomsday Beast, is no match...

t_ch: 可以这么说，但劝你不要把这事挂在嘴上——引来不必要的关注可不好。
t_en: You're not wrong, but be careful about blurting things out loud — it could attract unwanted attention.

t_ch: 我是谁，我在哪…
t_en: Who am I, where am I...

t_ch: 你是{NICKNAME}，这里是黑塔空间站。
t_en: Well, this is the Herta Space Station.

t_ch: 歇一会儿吧，不打扰你了。对了，把你的信标地址给我——有什么事，好联系上你。
t_en: I guess I'll leave you to rest up a bit for the time being. By the way, what's your beacon address? That way if anything happens, I can contact you.

t_ch: 你和丹恒交换了手机号码。
t_en: You and Dan Heng exchanged phone numbers.

t_ch: 方便的话去见一下姬子，她说有事找你。
t_en: Go see Himeko when you have the time. She said she had something to discuss with you.

t_ch: 我还能回空间站吗？
t_en: Can I still return to the space station?

t_ch: 当然能呀，我还等着你呢——噢，你说的「回」是——噢，倒也不是不行。
t_en: Of course, I'll still be here waiting for you, eh... and the space station will be waiting for you. Returning... it's not impossible.

t_ch: 你愿意在这儿当个普通科员的话，可以问问艾丝妲，她同意我就没意见。
t_en: If you wanna be a researcher here, you can just ask Asta. If she's fine with it, I have no complaints.

t_ch: 不，等下——你身体有颗星核啊——你还是走吧，偶尔回来瞧瞧，久住还是太危险了。
t_en: No, hold on — you have a Stellaron within you. I think it's better if you left and came back occasionally. It might be dangerous if you stay for too long...

t_ch: 我可以留在空间站吗？
t_en: Can I stay at the space station?

t_ch: 不可以，你太危险了。虽然我不在乎，但总得替艾丝妲她们想想。
t_en: No. You're way too dangerous. I don't mind personally, but I don't want Asta and others to take the risk.

t_ch: 你也不想因为自己身体里埋了颗星核，祸害空间站那么多人吧？
t_en: You wouldn't want the Stellaron in you to hurt all the people in the space station either, right?

t_ch: 偶尔回来看看就好，空间站永远欢迎你——尽量别待太久。
t_en: Just come back once in a while. You will always be welcome at the space station — just don't stay for too long.

t_ch: 我真的很想留在空间站。
t_en: I really want to stay.

t_ch: …你真的很怪哎。这个地方有什么好的？我都不怎么乐意待在这儿……
t_en: ...You really are weird. What's so good about this place? Even I don't like staying here...

t_ch: 行吧。说实话，区区星核，真要封住，我也有得是办法——就是你可能不大自由而已。
t_en: Ugh. Fine. To be honest with you, I have plenty of ways to seal a mere Stellaron — You just might end up not having a lot of personal freedom.

t_ch: 既然你一心想留下来…好吧，我不反对。你找艾丝妲吧，她是站长，她说了算。
t_en: If you're so keen to stay... Fine. I'm okay with it. Go speak to Asta. She's the lead researcher. She's in charge.

t_ch: 我能留在空间站吗？
t_en: Can I stay at the space station?

t_ch: 不是回答过了嘛！你问艾丝妲！她是站长，她说了算。
t_en: As I've said, talk to Asta! She's the lead researcher. She's in charge. Go, go, go.

t_ch: 黑塔说我可以留下。
t_en: Herta says I can stay.

t_ch: 哎？黑塔女士这么说？
t_en: Huh? Madam Herta said that?

t_ch: 既然她都表态了…我也没有反对的理由啦。不过我要提醒你，{NICKNAME}，如果黑塔女士要对你做什么的话……
t_en: In that case... I have nothing against it either. Mind you, if Madam Herta was to do something to you, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 她可是不太在乎你的想法喔。
t_en: She's not going to care a lot about what you think.

t_ch: 我决定留在太空站。
t_en: I've made up my mind. I'll stay.

t_ch: 咦？可是黑塔……
t_en: Stay? But Herta...

t_ch: 黑塔和艾丝妲都同意了。
t_en: Herta and Asta both gave me the green light.

t_ch: 这样啊…那么，我也尊重你的选择。
t_en: Is that so... Well, I respect your choice.

t_ch: 要好好保重哦，{NICKNAME}，我们会经常回来看你的。
t_en: Take care, {NICKNAME}. We'll come back now and then to see you.

t_ch: 再见，姬子女士。
t_en: So long, Himeko.

t_ch: 我还得再想想……
t_en: I need some more time to think...

t_ch: 经过慎重考虑，你决定谢绝姬子的邀请。汽笛嗡鸣，星穹列车即将启航，萍水相逢的友人们向你挥手致意。离别正如相遇一般突然而坚定。
t_en: After careful deliberation, you decide to decline Himeko's invitation. With a long whistle, the Astral Express prepares to go ahead on its journey. The friends you just made wave goodbye to you, and your farewell is as sudden and final as your meeting.

t_ch: 星穹列车离去后，空间站如火如荼地启动重建工作，而你与黑塔则开始了对星核的研究。但很快，黑塔就对你和星核都失去了兴趣。在艾丝妲的安排下，你成为空间站的一名科员。
t_en: After the departure of the Express, the station begins to eagerly rebuild. Meanwhile, Herta and you get busy while researching the Stellaron. However, it doesn't take long before Herta loses interest in you and the Stellaron, and Asta arranges for you to become a researcher at the space station.

t_ch: 在「黑塔」，你度过了平静的一生。星穹列车返回数次，就再也没有了消息。从来往空间站的旅行者口中，你听说了星穹列车与星核猎手那震动寰宇的结局。有时，你不禁思忖如果自己踏上那辆列车，他们的未来会否有所不同。
t_en: You spend a peaceful life on the space station. The Express visits a few times before disappearing for good. From the occasional visitors on the station, you hear stories about how the final end of the Express and the Stellaron Hunters shakes the universe to its very core. Sometimes you wonder whether their destiny would have changed, had you stepped onto that train.

t_ch: 不过，那已经与你无关了。
t_en: But those stories are no longer for you.

t_ch: 《崩坏：星穹铁道》
t_en: Honkai: Star Rail

t_ch: 领衔主演\n{NICKNAME}
t_en: Lead Actors\n{NICKNAME}

t_ch:
主演
 丹恒  饰  丹恒
？？？ 饰  三月七
 姬子  饰  姬子
 约阿希姆•诺基安维塔宁  饰  瓦尔特•杨
t_en:
Starring
Dan Heng  as  DAN HENG
???  as  MARCH 7TH
Himeko  as  HIMEKO
Joachim Nokianvirtanen  as  WELT YANG

t_ch: 友情出演\n卡芙卡  银狼\n艾丝妲  阿兰
t_en: With cameo appearances by\nKafka  Silver Wolf\nAsta  Arlan

t_ch: 特别鸣谢\n黑塔\n反物质军团
t_en: Special thanks expressed to\nHerta\nthe Antimatter Legion

t_ch: 剧本指导\n艾利欧
t_en: Script Supervisor\nElio

t_ch: 愿此行，终抵群星。
t_en: May this journey lead us starward.

t_ch: 看起来傻头傻脑的。没错，叫的就是你。
t_en: Hey, you over there looking dumbfounded. Yes, Pom-Pom's talking to you.

t_ch: 具体情况我已经从姬子那儿听说了。听好了新人，重要的事情我只说一遍。
t_en: Himeko told Pom-Pom about your situation. Now listen up, Pom-Pom will only say this once.

t_ch: 最近应该有不少人都会这么跟你说：「你是特殊的」。但这里是星穹列车，车上的乘客多少都沾点不能说的秘密。
t_en: Pom-Pom's sure there have been lots of people telling you how special you are lately, but this is the Astral Express, and everyone on here has their secrets.

t_ch: 既然选择了上车，就得遵守这里的规矩。特殊的并不只你一个，这点你可给我记好了。
t_en: Since you chose to board, you can abide by the rules. You're not the only special one here. You'd best remember that.

t_ch: 我是这儿的列车长帕姆，在车上遇到任何问题你都尽管来找我吧。
t_en: I'm Pom-Pom, the conductor. Just come find me if you have any trouble.

t_ch: 喔，是你呀。感觉怎么样？
t_en: Oh, it's you. How do you feel?

t_ch: 很有精神。
t_en: Full of energy.

t_ch: 那就好，看来你的体质真的很特别。
t_en: Great. Looks like your stamina is really quite special.

t_ch: 我本以为还要在空间站多盘桓些时日，看来你的体质的确有特殊之处。
t_en: I thought I'd have to stay in the space station a few more days, but there does seem something special about you.

t_ch: 浑身头疼。
t_en: I've got a killer headache.

t_ch: …待会儿我来给你检查一下。
t_en: ...I'll check you out in a minute.

t_ch: 你的身体被星核猎手动过手脚，也许是留下了什么后遗症。
t_en: Your body has been tampered with by the Stellaron Hunters. Perhaps that left behind some aftereffects.

t_ch: 很有品味。
t_en: Very tasteful.

t_ch: 品位…啊，你说这辆列车？我是在问你的身体状况。
t_en: Tasteful... Oh, you mean the train? I was asking how you felt physically.

t_ch: 不过的确，从内饰来看，「开拓」的品位还挺不错的。
t_en: You are right, though. Looking at the interior decoration, you can tell that trailblazers have quite good taste.

t_ch: 总之，谢谢你救了小三月一回。
t_en: In any case, I have to thank you for saving March.

t_ch: 你们也救了我啊。
t_en: You guys saved me, too.

t_ch: 唔，其实也只是让你体内的东西暂时安静下来……
t_en: Heh, all I did was calm that thing inside you down temporarily...

t_ch: 不是想吓唬你，但说实话：只要它还在你体内，就谈不上获救。
t_en: ...I don't want to frighten you, but the truth is you won't ever be in the clear while it is still inside your body.

t_ch: 不能袖手旁观吧。
t_en: I couldn't just stand by and watch.

t_ch: 你能这么想，这很好。拥有这样的想法并付诸行动的人，是真正意义上的英雄。
t_en: It's good that you think so. Someone with that mindset and the actions to back it up — that is the true meaning of the word "hero."

t_ch: 其实没想那么多…
t_en: I didn't think too much about it...

t_ch: 不假思索地做出善举——这是英雄最重要的资质。
t_en: Helping others without a second thought — that is the most important quality of a hero.

t_ch: 但是，只要「星核」还在你身体里，就要小心行事。我和姬子没有把握每次都能恰好压制住它。
t_en: However, as long as the Stellaron is still in your body, you should be careful what you do. I don't know if Himeko and I can suppress it again.

t_ch: 沉闷的话题就聊到这里吧。空间站里发生的变故太多，想必你也累了。
t_en: But I won't bore you any longer. So much happened at the space station, you must be tired.

t_ch: 距离列车跃迁应该还有一些时间，随意走走熟悉一下环境吧。
t_en: There should be some time until the next warp jump, so feel free to walk around and familiarize yourself with the environment.

t_ch: 准备好前往下一个星球了吗？
t_en: Are you ready to move on to the next planet?

t_ch: 下一站，会去哪里呢？
t_en: What's the next stop for the Express?

t_ch: 先等等…
t_en: Wait...

t_ch: 感觉如何？和你想象中的星穹列车有什么区别么？
t_en: What do you think? Does the Astral Express look the same as you imagined?

t_ch: 果然是列车的样子。
t_en: As I expected, it looks like a train.

t_ch: 每个来到列车的人，都是这里的乘客。
t_en: Everyone on the Express is a passenger.

t_ch: 大家向未知的终点奔赴，就像是在旅行一样。
t_en: We're all heading towards an unknown destination, like we are traveling together.

t_ch: 可能正是因为如此，「开拓」才选择了这样的外观吧？
t_en: Maybe that's why the Trailblaze chose such a look.

t_ch: 总感觉缺了点什么……
t_en: I feel like it's missing something...

t_ch: 小三月最开始也这么说过。
t_en: March said something like that at first, too.

t_ch: 看来年轻人的口味，就是要相似些。
t_en: Seems like you young people have similar tastes.

t_ch: 对了，三月和丹恒现在应该待在自己的房间里。
t_en: Oh right — March and Dan Heng should both be in their rooms right now.

t_ch: 你可以去找他们哦，年轻人融洽相处吧。
t_en: You can go look for them. You youngsters should get along well.

t_ch: 房间好玩吗？
t_en: Are their rooms fun?

t_ch: 咳，这个，年轻人嘛，房间自然都比较有个性一点。
t_en: Ah, you know, young people! Their rooms always reflect their personality.

t_ch: 你可以先去…习惯习惯？
t_en: You can go and... have a look around?

t_ch: 那这里呢…
t_en: What about here...?

t_ch: 这里是我们平时集会的所在，后面的车厢才是用来休息的私人空间。
t_en: We usually meet up here, but our personal cabins are in the next carriage.

t_ch: 对了，别看帕姆那副样子，列车很久没有新乘客了，它对你还是很感兴趣的。
t_en: Also, don't mind Pom-Pom's antics. They're actually pretty interested in you. It's just that we haven't had new passengers on the Express for a long while.

t_ch: 我就不越俎代庖啦。有什么想问的，尽管去麻烦它这个列车长好了。
t_en: I won't steal Pom-Pom's thunder. If you have any questions, just go ask our conductor.

t_ch: 这么快就来了？我就是客气客气帕…有什么事嘛？
t_en: Here already? I was just engaging in pleasantries. How can I help?

t_ch: 三月七的房间怎么走？
t_en: Where's March 7th's room?

t_ch: 嗯？你这么关心她的房间干什么？
t_en: Oh? Why are you interested in her room?

t_ch: 啊，我听姬子说之前是你救了三月七乘客？
t_en: Ah, Pom-Pom remembers Himeko saying that you saved her...

t_ch: 唔呣，勇气可嘉，就是有点太鲁莽了。但这倒的确是有点开拓者该有的样子。
t_en: Mmm, very brave! Very foolhardy! But that is what a Trailblazer should be like.

t_ch: 三月七乘客的房间就在客车车厢。不过她总喜欢到处乱跑，不一定在就是了。
t_en: March 7th's room is in the Express Sleeper Compartment. She's always running around, so she might not be there.

t_ch: 丹恒的房间……
t_en: About Dan Heng's room...

t_ch: 丹恒乘客的房间？哦，你说的是资料室帕。
t_en: Dan Heng's room? Oh, you mean the archives.

t_ch: 他只是…单纯地赖在里边不走罢了，我也懒得管他。
t_en: He's just... sort of living in there, I guess. I can't be bothered getting him out.

t_ch: 三月七乘客的房间就在资料室的隔壁，你顺路倒是可以去看看他。
t_en: March 7th's room is right next to the Archives. You can visit him on the way.

t_ch: 说起来那是…留声机？
t_en: Is that a... phonograph?

t_ch: 你居然也认得那东西？
t_en: You recognize this as well?

t_ch: 姬子总喜欢在各种地方捡些奇奇怪怪的破烂玩意儿回来修理。
t_en: Himeko always likes to bring back weird junk and try to fix it.

t_ch: 那一件好像还被做了点改装。
t_en: That also got modified a bit.

t_ch: 这里还有自动售货机？
t_en: There's a vending machine too?

t_ch: 「星际和平公司」的售货机在哪儿看见都没什么好奇怪的帕？
t_en: Shouldn't be a surprise to see the IPC's vending machine anywhere...

t_ch: 虽然不想承认，但他们的商品种类确实齐全。
t_en: Pom-Pom doesn't wanna admit it, but they really do have everything you may need on sale.

t_ch: 有这么个东西，或许能给旅途带来些许调剂。
t_en: This thing might add some nice flavor to the trip.

t_ch: 今天暂时就问到这里了。
t_en: That's all my questions for today.

t_ch: 列车跃迁还有些准备工作要做。我暂时抽不开身，你就自己到处走走吧。
t_en: Pom-Pom still needs to prepare for the Express' warp jump. You can look around the place yourself.

t_ch: 放心，在这个列车上，你的一举一动，都逃不过帕姆的眼睛。
t_en: No matter where you go on this train, Pom-Pom will always have my eyes on you.

t_ch: 姬子很喜欢听留声机，说能播放来自过去的旋律。
t_en: Himeko likes using the Phonograph a lot. She says it can play melodies from the past.

t_ch: 瓦尔特很喜欢收集这些黑不溜秋的圆盘，似乎是古董货。你要能带几张回来，他应该会很高兴的。
t_en: Welt likes collecting these jet-black discs. It seems like they could be antiques. He'd be very happy if you could bring a few back.

t_ch: 「星际和平公司」的售后部还是很周全的帕，自动售货机里的商品种类不仅丰富还会定期上新。
t_en: The Interastral Peace Corporation's after-sales service is very comprehensive. The vending machine has a wide range of products that are regularly refreshed.

t_ch: 超距传输很贵的，不过这台机子是从黑塔女士那儿弄来的，商品全都包邮帕。
t_en: Hyper-distant delivery costs an arm and a leg, but we got this machine from Madam Herta, all the products inside come free of shipping charges.

t_ch: 喂！你、你还真就一点问题都没有想问我的吗？！
t_en: Hey! Y—You don't have any questions to ask Pom-Pom!?

t_ch: 难道是嫌我啰嗦吗？还、还是说觉得和我这个年龄段的聊天已经有代沟了？！
t_en: Is it because you think Pom-Pom talks too much? Or is it Pom-Pom's age? You youngsters gotta learn to bridge that generation gap.

t_ch: 你怎么刚走就又回来了？！
t_en: You just left. Why are you back again!?

t_ch: 快去快去，我这儿忙着呢，可没那么多闲工夫陪你。
t_en: Go, go. I'm busy here and I don't have time for you.

t_ch: （似乎能听到电子仪器的声音……）
t_en: (There seems to be the sound of electronic equipment...)

t_ch: ——什么人？
t_en: Hm? Who's there?

t_ch: 是我，{NICKNAME}
t_en: It's me, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: …是你啊。门没锁，进来吧。
t_en: ...Oh, it's you. The door's not locked. Come in.

t_ch: 我这都还没敲门呢……
t_en: I haven't even knocked...

t_ch: 被人破门而入的次数多了，自然就会感知气息了。
t_en: I learned to sense incoming visitors after people kept barging in so frequently.

t_ch: 有什么事？
t_en: Can I help you?

t_ch: 闲逛而已。
t_en: Just looking around.

t_ch: 随意，这里对列车上的所有人开放。
t_en: Feel free. This is open to everyone on the Express.

t_ch: 看看你家。
t_en: I came to visit your pad.

t_ch: 这不是我家，只是睡觉的地方。
t_en: This is not my pad, it's just where I sleep.

t_ch: 它是资料室，星穹列车从初次发车到现在，全部的行车记录都存在这个房间里。
t_en: These are the Archives. It has the records of all the Astral Express's trips since its first journey.

t_ch: 你在整理数据？
t_en: Are you sorting out the data?

t_ch: 嗯，个人兴趣。
t_en: Yes, just my personal interest.

t_ch: 除了整理已有数据，我也会保存本次旅行的档案。
t_en: Aside from the existing data, I also recorded this trip to the archives.

t_ch: 你自己去看吧，我不清楚你对「好玩」的定义。
t_en: Go look them up yourself. I don't know your definition of "fun."

t_ch: 看见自己的名字也别吃惊，我会保存本次旅行的档案。
t_en: If you see your own name, don't be surprised, I also recorded this trip to the archives.

t_ch: 虽然很多数据损坏了，但残存的零星片段也有难以消化的庞大信息量，我正在尝试整理。
t_en: A lot of the data is corrupted, but even the residual pieces contain huge amounts of information that we're struggling to process. I'm trying to sort them out.

t_ch: 讲几个好玩的？
t_en: Any fun ones?

t_ch: 即便阿基维利的旅途大多已不可考，至少作为列车现在的乘客，将我们的见闻记录下来是有其意义的。
t_en: While many of the roads that Akivili traveled along no longer exist, I think it's still meaningful to record our adventures as current passengers of the Express.

t_ch: 所以我把收集到的资料输入资料室的「智库」，并将列车所过之地的人文生态、地质特征等等逐一整理录入并与过去的数据比对印证。
t_en: I enter the collected data into the archives' data bank. I try to catalog the people and places the Express encounters, and compare and contrast them with the existing records.

t_ch: 看见那边的终端了么？它能连接智库调阅目前已知的情报，你可以试试。
t_en: Do you see the terminal over there? It can be used to view information already stored in the data bank. Do give it a go.

t_ch: 之后旅行中的见闻，我也会收录整合进去，你可以时常来看看。我们的终端设备上也有快捷入口。
t_en: I'll be entering any new information we encounter on future journeys, so drop by anytime to check it out. We also have a shortcut on the terminal device.

t_ch: 没想到你还是个学者
t_en: I didn't take you to be a scholar.

t_ch: 你对我的误解还挺深。算了，我知道是谁干的。
t_en: Seems like you have some deep misunderstandings about me. *sigh* Ah, well, I think I know who caused that.

t_ch: 没想到你电脑修得这么好。
t_en: I didn't know you could fix computers so well.

t_ch: 我不是…唉……
t_en: Y'know, I'm not... *sigh*

t_ch: 我还有事，你自便。
t_en: I have matters to attend to, feel free.

t_ch: 对了，你要去隔壁的话……
t_en: So, if you're headed next door...

t_ch: …小点声。
t_en: ...keep it down.

t_ch: …要跃迁了吗？你先去吧。
t_en: ...Is it time to warp jump? You go ahead.

t_ch: 整齐码放在书架上的纸质资料，如丹恒所言内容非常冗杂，甚至有许多手写稿件。
t_en: Paperback documents are placed tidily on the shelf. Like Dan Heng said, the content is very dense, and some of it is even handwritten.

t_ch: 其中有一些文字难以解读，图画也晦涩难懂，想要深入理解看来非一朝一夕能够解读……
t_en: Some of the words are barely legible, and illustrations faded beyond recognition. It would take many moons for one to decipher their contents.

t_ch: 被褥只有薄薄一层，但看上去柔软暖和。被包围在资料书刊之中，意外地有安全感。
t_en: There is only one thin quilt, but it looks very warm. Being surrounded by all the books and records provides a surprising sense of security.

t_ch: （不过这么硬的床…真的可以好好休息吗？）
t_en: (But... can he really sleep on such a hard bed?)

t_ch: 硬床板对脊椎有好处，床铺太过柔软反而不好。
t_en: A hard bed is good for your spine. A bed that's too soft will end up hurting you.

t_ch: （他怎么知道的…难道是眼神出卖了我？！）
t_en: (How did he know... Did my gaze betray me!?)

t_ch: 随意搭在椅子上的衣服，给这间资料室增添了些许生活的气息。
t_en: Clothes casually flung on the chair add a touch of life to this database room.

t_ch: （不知道他是不是只有这一种款式的衣服……）
t_en: (I wonder if this is the only style of clothes he owns...)

t_ch: 你敲敲门，里边没什么反应。
t_en: (You knock, but there's no response...)

t_ch: （门没有锁，要不要进去看看呢……）
t_en: (The door is unlocked. Should I go in?)

t_ch: 稍微看看吧？
t_en: Just a little peek?

t_ch: （只是稍微看一看的话…应该没有问题吧？）
t_en: (Just one look should be fine, right?)

t_ch: 还是算了吧。
t_en: Never mind.

t_ch: （还是等房间主人回来再说吧。）
t_en: (Better to wait till the room's owner comes back.)

t_ch: （这些都是…三月七拍的？）
t_en: (Did March 7th take all these pictures...?)

t_ch: （甚至连之前空间站的照片都有……）
t_en: (There are even pictures of the space station...)

t_ch: 做出不让自己后悔的选择…抵达未来……
t_en: Make a choice I won't regret... reach the future...

t_ch: 总感觉在很短的时间里，发生了很多的事。
t_en: (So much has happened in such a short time...)

t_ch: 来到这里…真的是正确的选择么？
t_en: Coming on board the Express... Was it really the right decision?

t_ch: （喂——喂喂——）各位乘客请注意，各位乘客请注意。
t_en: Ahem, hi, hello~ Attention all passengers. Attention all passengers.

t_ch: 列车跃迁在即，请各位速至列车大厅集合。
t_en: The Express is about to conduct a warp jump. All passengers, please gather at the main hall.

t_ch: 重复一遍。列车跃迁在即，请各位速至列车大厅集合。
t_en: I repeat. The Express is about to conduct a warp jump. All passengers, please gather at the main hall.

t_ch: 门被锁住，但是门内隐约透出一缕奇特的咖啡清香。
t_en: The door is locked, but there's a peculiar scent of coffee coming out of the room.

t_ch: 门像是和这个空间固定在了一起，纹丝不动。
t_en: The door doesn't even move an inch, as if it is glued in space.

t_ch: （看样子真的很喜欢摄影啊……）
t_en: (She really does love photography...)

t_ch: （不知道培育的是什么植物，下次问问看三月七好了。）
t_en: (I can't tell what plant this is. Maybe I'll ask March 7th next time.)

t_ch: （这么大的玩偶放在床上，晚上睡觉真的不会觉得拥挤吗？）
t_en: (With such a huge doll on the bed, wouldn't it feel really cramped at night?)

t_ch: （应该是列车旅程中的纪念品…吧？应该是这样的吧。）
t_en: (This looks like a souvenir from the journeys... right? Probably.)

t_ch: 你来啦！马上就要前往下一站了，是不是感觉很兴奋？
t_en: There you are!

t_ch: 对哦，这还是你第一次的旅行，那…应该是双倍的兴奋？
t_en: Wait, this is your first trip! So that should be double the excitement, right?

t_ch: 我准备好了，我准备好了！
t_en: I'm ready, I'm ready to go!

t_ch: 很好，很有精神嘛！
t_en: That's the spirit!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我第一次经历跃迁的时候，也很激动。但现在我可就稳重多啦！
t_en: Hehe, I was excited the first time I experienced a warp jump, too. But I'm used to it now.

t_ch: 放心，很快你就会习惯起来，变成和我一样成熟靠谱的乘客的。嗯。
t_en: Don't worry, you'll get used to it too, and before you know it you'll be a mature and dependable passenger just like me.

t_ch: 但对未来…还是稍微有点担心。
t_en: But... I'm still a little worried about what lies ahead.
